name: Penn State Golf Courses
address: 1523 W College Ave, State College, PA 16801
about: Penn State Golf Courses is a dual-course facility featuring the Blue Course and White Course, both championship-level layouts. The Blue Course was renovated in 2006, adding three ponds and extending over 640 yards, while the White Course, with holes dating back to 1926, offers a traditional challenge. The venue includes an indoor golf simulator with three bays available for play, as well as a golf shop, practice facilities, and the 19th Hole at the Walker Clubhouse. It hosts a variety of events, including trivia nights, leagues, and outings.
why_golfers_like_it: Two championship golf courses with unique layouts and scenic views
Indoor golf simulators available for weather-proof play and practice
Extensive event hosting capabilities including leagues and corporate outings
On-site dining at the 19th Hole and full golf shop amenities

food_and_drink.notes: Full menu available at the 19th Hole of the Walker Clubhouse
Full bar service available at the 19th Hole

faq.question: Do I need my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, players are expected to bring their own golf clubs to use on the courses and simulators.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: Outside food and drinks are not permitted, but the 19th Hole offers a full menu and bar service.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: 18 holes on the simulators take approximately 60 minutes for one person, depending on pace of play.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue is family-friendly and offers junior golf programs and youth instruction.
